name: Bistro Laaroussa
quote: serves home-style Moroccan specialties and Mediterranean dishes, everything prepared with fresh and local ingredients
article_title: Eating Well in Fez, Morocco

name: The Ruined Garden
quote: Modern Moroccan dinners include a seven-hour lamb 'Mechwi' or, my favorite, a yummy chicken pastilla
article_title: Eating Well in Fez, Morocco

name: Restaurant Almandar
quote: menu includes tagines, pastilla, couscous, and a range of 'fast foods' Moroccan style, such as paninis, burgers, and grilled roast chicken
article_title: Eating Well in Fez, Morocco

name: Café Clock
quote: fusion dishes please the palates of locals and travelers alike. The Clock offers a uniquely seasoned camel burger as the house specialty
article_title: Eating Well in Fez, Morocco

name: Ishq Restaurant
dishes_mentioned: black couscous with caramelized pears and beef
quote: fine-dining menu of Moroccan fusion cuisine presents original takes on local flavors and Western dishes
article_title: Eating Well in Fez, Morocco

name: La Maison Bleue
quote: one of Fes' few fine dining restaurants set in an intimate space at an eponymous riad, offering a prix fixe menu at 550 dhs per person including starter, main course, dessert, coffee and house wine
article_title: 15 BEST Restaurants in Fes, Morocco: Must-Try Spots for Foodies

name: Darori Resto
quote: beautiful space covered in zellij and accented with carved plaster, humble Darori is a celebration of Fassi staples like beef and prune tagine
article_title: 15 BEST Restaurants in Fes, Morocco: Must-Try Spots for Foodies

name: Dar Roumana
dishes_mentioned: beetroot pear and honey balsamic salad with local goat cheese
quote: Franco-Moroccan fusion restaurant where Moroccan chef Youness Toumi leverages local produce to illustrate the seasons of Fes' medina with elegance
article_title: 15 BEST Restaurants in Fes, Morocco: Must-Try Spots for Foodies

name: Dar Hammad
quote: rooftop restaurant in Fes serves some of the city's best authentic Moroccan food while showcasing one of the best views of Fes from above
article_title: 15 BEST Restaurants in Fes, Morocco: Must-Try Spots for Foodies

name: Fondouk Bazaar
quote: trendy, artsy hideaway is a favorite in Fes for its fusion cuisine pulling inspiration from the Mediterranean, Middle East, and Morocco, and is incredibly vegetarian-friendly
article_title: 15 BEST Restaurants in Fes, Morocco: Must-Try Spots for Foodies

name: The Ruined Garden
quote: lush courtyard restaurant in Fes el Bali is one of the city's most Instagrammable, feeling like an effortlessly chic step into a stylish tastemaker's home
article_title: 15 BEST Restaurants in Fes, Morocco: Must-Try Spots for Foodies

name: Mom's Touch
dishes_mentioned: Eggplant Cannelloni with ras el hanout tomato sauce
quote: cozy rooftop restaurant is an inviting, bohemian space serving Moroccan and Middle Eastern favorites including Bida Matisha (shakshouka) and Eggplant Cannelloni in a ras el hanout tomato sauce
article_title: 15 BEST Restaurants in Fes, Morocco: Must-Try Spots for Foodies

name: Café Clock
dishes_mentioned: Couscous Boohloo with caramelized raisins and apricots
quote: rooftop terrace in the medina is best known for the viral camel burger (a social media darling), though its more mainstream fare tends to be more attractive to diners
article_title: 15 BEST Restaurants in Fes, Morocco: Must-Try Spots for Foodies

name: La Cave
quote: Moroccan and International restaurant in Fes' medina offering pasta, Asian food like spring rolls, and Turkish cuisine, while reviewers applaud some of the best Moroccan food in the city
article_title: 15 BEST Restaurants in Fes, Morocco: Must-Try Spots for Foodies

name: Le Peroke Cafe Restaurant
quote: rooftop offers an incredible view of the Marinid Tombs and Fes el Bali, designed to be enjoyed by settling into plush, lounge-style seating, with hospitable owner Yassir and welcoming waitstaff
article_title: 15 BEST Restaurants in Fes, Morocco: Must-Try Spots for Foodies

name: Le Tarbouche
quote: petite eatery frequently filled to the brim with tourists enjoying Fassi favorites, with vegetable pastilla at just 40 dhs and a starter of four Moroccan salads at 30 dhs
article_title: 15 BEST Restaurants in Fes, Morocco: Must-Try Spots for Foodies

name: Restaurant Dar Hatim
quote: exquisitely tiled and wonderfully ornate restaurant feels more regal than homey, with prix fixe menus from 220 to 300 dhs opening with Moroccan salads and a choice of pastilla fassie, steamed lamb, or lamb tagine
article_title: 15 BEST Restaurants in Fes, Morocco: Must-Try Spots for Foodies

name: Café Restaurant Al Oud
quote: small, colorful restaurant opening right onto a main medina street, where travelers rave about some of the best pastillas and lamb tagine in the medina, with tagines from 70 to 85 dhs
article_title: 15 BEST Restaurants in Fes, Morocco: Must-Try Spots for Foodies

name: Fez Café
quote: backyard garden overflows with greenery, accented by an eclectic array of decor pulling on novelty Moroccan pop culture influences, serving Moroccan and international cuisine accented by Mediterranean flavor
article_title: 15 BEST Restaurants in Fes, Morocco: Must-Try Spots for Foodies

name: Restaurant Gayza
quote: la cheffe Lalla Abida perpétue un savoir-faire transmis de mère en fille, revisitant les plats traditionnels marocains avec des spécialités comme la pastilla aux fruits de mer et le tagine de fruits épicés
article_title: 15 meilleurs restaurants à Fès (2026): nos avis

name: Ishq Restaurant
dishes_mentioned: velouté poivron jaune ananas fromage de chèvre sauce pistou
souris d'agneau aux oignons caramélisés et poire vapeur
pastilla au lait glace corne de gazelle amandes fleur d'oranger
quote: cuisine fusion alliant avec subtilité les ingrédients: velouté poivron jaune ananas fromage de chèvre sauce pistou, souris d'agneau aux oignons caramélisés et poire vapeur, pastilla au lait glace corne de gazelle
article_title: 15 meilleurs restaurants à Fès (2026): nos avis

name: Restaurant Lounge MB
dishes_mentioned: filet de bœuf Rossini rôti dans son jus
pageot royal rôti aux légumes à la chermoula
quote: fusion du terroir marocain au savoir-faire français, avec au menu le filet de bœuf Rossini rôti dans son jus, le pageot royal rôti aux légumes à la chermoula, et le Saint-Pierre sauvage rôti sur risotto
article_title: 15 meilleurs restaurants à Fès (2026): nos avis

name: Café-restaurant Moulay Idriss
quote: couscous de bœuf et le thé à la menthe sont servis par les propriétaires Siham et Driss dans un cadre qui respire l'authenticité et la profondeur des saveurs fassies
article_title: 15 meilleurs restaurants à Fès (2026): nos avis

name: Le Tarbouche
quote: la pastilla au poulet mérite une mention spéciale dans ce restaurant aux influences marocaines, méditerranéennes et tex-mex tenu par Fadoua et Jacob
article_title: 15 meilleurs restaurants à Fès (2026): nos avis

name: Restaurant Palais Laraichi
dishes_mentioned: poissons de mer et de lac
quote: onze types de viandes proposés — veau, agneau, chameau, poissons de mer et de lac, poulet, poulet fermier, lapin, pigeon, autruche et perdrix — dans un cadre de zellige et boiseries centenaires
article_title: 15 meilleurs restaurants à Fès (2026): nos avis

name: La Morille Dar Diafa
quote: assortiment de salades marocaines, tagines comme le poulet fassi préparé comme à la maison, tenjia ou tanjia servis dans un espace sobre et aéré face à la Wilaya
article_title: 15 meilleurs restaurants à Fès (2026): nos avis

name: Restaurant L'Amandier
dishes_mentioned: crème de lentilles vertes et escalope de foie gras poêlé
tarte fine au chèvre et élixir d'aubergines
quote: crème de lentilles vertes et escalope de foie gras poêlé, tarte fine au chèvre et élixir d'aubergines, Rfissa au coquelet fermier — inspirés des cuisines arabes, berbères et juives de Fès
article_title: 15 meilleurs restaurants à Fès (2026): nos avis

name: Mom's Touch
quote: briouates, couscous de poulet et burger de chameau proposés entre 40 et 90 MAD dans ce restaurant multi-terrasses de la médina
article_title: 15 meilleurs restaurants à Fès (2026): nos avis

name: Restaurant Benyamna
quote: tajines mijotés et couscous aux sept légumes préparés par la mère aux fourneaux, aidée de ses filles Yasmina et Sabrina et de leur frère Abdel, près de la tannerie Chouara
article_title: 15 meilleurs restaurants à Fès (2026): nos avis

name: Bistrot des Saveurs
dishes_mentioned: sandwich au pain marocain maison garni de viande hachée
quote: sandwich au pain marocain maison garni de viande hachée, salade gambas César et jus détox proposés autour de 170 MAD dans ce bistrot de la ville nouvelle
article_title: 15 meilleurs restaurants à Fès (2026): nos avis
